# Plan 13: Local file-based task tracking as alternative to Linear

## Context
The code-flow plugin currently depends on Linear for all task state tracking. This means users can't try the workflow without a Linear account and the Linear MCP server configured. Adding a local file-based alternative makes the plugin accessible to anyone with just Claude Code.

**Linear feature issue:** PB-221

## Design decisions

- **Format: XML** — machine-writable, extensible, not whitespace-sensitive (unlike YAML). Human readability deferred to a future viewer tool.
- **File location: `.agents/plans/plan-{N}-tasks.xml`** — sibling to the plan file. Keeps the flat directory structure, no migration needed for existing plans, compatible with existing git tag conventions.
- **Scripts: TypeScript, pre-compiled to JS** — TS source in `scripts/` in the plugin repo. SessionStart hook runs `tsc` once into `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA}/dist/`. Runtime invocation is plain `node`. TS source also copied to `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA}/src/` for browsing.
- **Dependencies: `typescript` + `fast-xml-parser`** (~26MB) — installed in `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA}` via SessionStart hook using the documented diff-and-install pattern.
- **Mode detection:** SKILL.md uses `[Linear]` / `[Local]` inline markers at divergence points. Unmarked instructions apply to both modes.

## XML schema

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<plan-tasks plan="13" description="Short description" plan-version="plan-13-v1">
  <metadata>
    <backlog-issue>PB-221</backlog-issue>
    <status>active</status> <!-- active | complete | abandoned | in-review -->
    <created>2026-04-09</created>
  </metadata>

  <tasks>
    <task id="1" risk="high" status="pending">
      <name>Task name here</name>
      <commit></commit>
      <created-from>plan-13-v1</created-from>
      <closed-at-version></closed-at-version>
      <comments>
        <comment timestamp="2026-04-09T14:00:00Z">De-risk validated: approach works.</comment>
      </comments>
      <deviations>
        <deviation type="minor" timestamp="2026-04-09T15:00:00Z">Split into two sub-tasks because...</deviation>
      </deviations>
    </task>
  </tasks>

  <project-updates>
    <update timestamp="2026-04-09T13:00:00Z">Plan ready for review.</update>
    <update timestamp="2026-04-09T16:00:00Z" blocked="true">Blocked: fundamental issue with approach X.</update>
  </project-updates>
</plan-tasks>
```

Valid task statuses: `pending`, `in-progress`, `de-risked`, `agent-coded`, `closed`, `needs-triage`, `abandoned`

## Linear operation → XML mapping

| # | Linear operation | XML equivalent |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | Create `[agent]` project | Create `plan-{N}-tasks.xml` with metadata |
| 2 | Create task issues | Add `<task>` elements |
| 3 | Update task status | Update `status` attribute on `<task>` |
| 4 | Post comment for review | Append `<comment>` to task |
| 5 | Record commit reference | Update `<commit>` element |
| 6 | Log minor deviation | Append `<deviation type="minor">` |
| 7 | Flag major deviation | Append `<update blocked="true">` at project level |
| 8 | Close task | Set status to `closed`, record closing version |
| 9 | Mark needs-triage | Set status to `needs-triage` |
| 10 | Complete/archive project | Set metadata status to `complete` |
| 11 | Record audit trail | `<created-from>` and `<closed-at-version>` per task |
| 12 | Post project update | Append `<update>` to `<project-updates>` |

## TypeScript scripts

Located at `scripts/tasks/` in the plugin repo. Each script handles one or more XML operations:

| Script | Operations covered | Example invocation |
|---|---|---|
| `init.ts` | #1, #2 | `node dist/init.js --plan 13 --tasks-from .agents/plans/plan-13.md` |
| `update-status.ts` | #3, #8, #9 | `node dist/update-status.js --plan 13 --task 2 --status agent-coded` |
| `add-comment.ts` | #4 | `node dist/add-comment.js --plan 13 --task 2 --message "De-risk validated"` |
| `set-commit.ts` | #5 | `node dist/set-commit.js --plan 13 --task 2 --commit a1b2c3d` |
| `add-deviation.ts` | #6 | `node dist/add-deviation.js --plan 13 --task 2 --type minor --message "Split task"` |
| `project-update.ts` | #7, #10, #12 | `node dist/project-update.js --plan 13 --message "Ready for review"` |
| `show.ts` | (read-only) | `node dist/show.js --plan 13` (pretty-print current state) |

All scripts read/write `.agents/plans/plan-{N}-tasks.xml` relative to the repo root.

## Plugin infrastructure

### package.json (in plugin repo root)
```json
{
  "dependencies": {
    "typescript": "^5.x",
    "fast-xml-parser": "^5.x"
  }
}
```

### tsconfig.json (in plugin repo root)
```json
{
  "compilerOptions": {
    "target": "ES2020",
    "module": "commonjs",
    "outDir": "${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA}/dist",
    "rootDir": "./scripts/tasks",
    "strict": true
  },
  "include": ["scripts/tasks/**/*.ts"]
}
```

### SessionStart hook (in hooks/hooks.json)
```json
{
  "hooks": {
    "SessionStart": [{
      "hooks": [{
        "type": "command",
        "command": "diff -q \"${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/package.json\" \"${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA}/package.json\" >/dev/null 2>&1 || (cd \"${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA}\" && cp \"${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/package.json\" . && npm install && cp \"${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/tsconfig.json\" . && npx tsc -p . && cp -r \"${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/scripts/tasks\" \"${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA}/src\") || rm -f \"${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA}/package.json\""
      }]
    }]
  }
}
```

### SKILL.md changes
- Add "Task Tracking Mode" section after Core Invariants
- Add `[Linear]`/`[Local]` markers at ~10 divergence points (enumerated in Linear operation mapping above)
- Update Core Invariant 3 for local mode: reference feature motivation in Context section
- Update Core Invariant 4: "Linear or the local tasks file tracks task state only"
- Update "What Lives Where" table with local mode row
- Update Repository Structure to show tasks.xml files

## Tasks

### Task 1: SessionStart hook — install deps and verify
- Add `package.json` with `typescript` + `fast-xml-parser` dependencies
- Create `hooks/hooks.json` with SessionStart hook using the diff-and-install pattern
- Hook installs deps into `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA}` and runs `tsc` on a minimal test file
- **Verification checkpoint:** rebuild dev plugin, start a new Claude session, confirm `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA}` contains `node_modules/`, compiled JS, and copied TS source
- This task gates everything else — no point building scripts if the hook doesn't work

**Default Risk: High** — first hook in this plugin, untested `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA}` pattern, and the entire plan depends on this working. If this fails, the approach needs rethinking.

### Task 2: Create TypeScript project infrastructure
- `tsconfig.json` targeting ES2020/commonjs
- `scripts/tasks/` directory structure
- Shared types/interfaces for the XML schema (task statuses, risk levels, etc.)

**Default Risk: Low** — standard project setup, no unknowns.

### Task 3: Implement `init.ts` — create tasks XML from plan file
- Parse plan markdown to extract task names (### Task N: pattern)
- Generate XML with proper schema, all tasks set to `pending`
- Accept risk levels as CLI args or default to unset

**Default Risk: Medium** — parsing plan markdown is the main uncertainty. Need to handle variations in plan file format.

### Task 4: Implement `update-status.ts` — change task status
- Parse XML, find task by id, update status attribute
- Validate status transitions (e.g., can't go from `closed` back to `pending`)
- Update `<closed-at-version>` when status becomes `closed`

**Default Risk: Low** — straightforward XML read/update/write.

### Task 5: Implement `add-comment.ts` and `add-deviation.ts`
- Append child elements to a task's comments/deviations sections
- Auto-generate ISO timestamps

**Default Risk: Low** — simple append operations.

### Task 6: Implement `set-commit.ts` and `project-update.ts`
- `set-commit`: update commit element on a task
- `project-update`: append to project-updates, handle `blocked` flag, update metadata status

**Default Risk: Low** — simple XML operations.

### Task 7: Implement `show.ts` — pretty-print task state
- Read XML and output a human-readable summary (task table + recent updates)
- Useful for the agent to inspect current state

**Default Risk: Low** — read-only, formatting only.

### Task 8: Update SKILL.md with `[Linear]`/`[Local]` mode markers
- Add "Task Tracking Mode" section
- Add markers at all 10+ divergence points
- Update Core Invariants 3 and 4
- Update "What Lives Where" table
- Update Repository Structure

**Default Risk: High** — largest change, highest risk of inconsistency. The SKILL.md is the contract for the entire workflow; errors here break the agent's behavior. Needs careful review.

### Task 9: Update README.md
- Note local task tracking as an alternative to Linear in Features section
- No new installation steps needed (SessionStart hook handles deps)

**Default Risk: Low** — small text change.

## Files to create/modify
- **Create:** `scripts/tasks/init.ts`, `update-status.ts`, `add-comment.ts`, `add-deviation.ts`, `set-commit.ts`, `project-update.ts`, `show.ts`, `types.ts`
- **Create:** `hooks/hooks.json`
- **Modify:** `package.json` — add dependencies
- **Create:** `tsconfig.json`
- **Modify:** `skills/code-flow/SKILL.md` — add local mode
- **Modify:** `README.md` — mention local mode

## Verification
1. Run `npm install` and `npx tsc` — confirm JS output compiles cleanly
2. Run `node dist/init.js --plan 99 --tasks-from .agents/plans/plan-12.md` — confirm valid XML generated
3. Run each script against the test XML — confirm operations produce valid XML
4. Run `mvn process-resources` — confirm SKILL.md with new sections copies to dev build
5. Enable the dev plugin, start a new session — confirm SessionStart hook installs deps and compiles TS
6. Read the modified SKILL.md end-to-end for logical consistency between Linear and Local modes

toolUseResult.content:
# Plan 13: Local file-based task tracking as alternative to Linear

## Context
The code-flow plugin currently depends on Linear for all task state tracking. This means users can't try the workflow without a Linear account and the Linear MCP server configured. Adding a local file-based alternative makes the plugin accessible to anyone with just Claude Code.

**Linear feature issue:** PB-221

## Design decisions

- **Format: XML** — machine-writable, extensible, not whitespace-sensitive (unlike YAML). Human readability deferred to a future viewer tool.
- **File location: `.agents/plans/plan-{N}-tasks.xml`** — sibling to the plan file. Keeps the flat directory structure, no migration needed for existing plans, compatible with existing git tag conventions.
- **Scripts: TypeScript, pre-compiled to JS** — TS source in `scripts/tasks/` in the plugin repo. SessionStart hook runs `tsc` once into `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA}/dist/`. Runtime invocation is plain `node`. TS source also copied to `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA}/src/` for browsing.
- **Dependencies: `typescript` + `fast-xml-parser`** (~26MB) — installed in `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA}` via SessionStart hook using the documented diff-and-install pattern from the [plugins reference](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/plugins-reference#persistent-data-directory).
- **Mode detection:** SKILL.md uses `[Linear]` / `[Local]` inline markers at divergence points. Unmarked instructions apply to both modes.

## XML schema

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<plan-tasks plan="13" description="Short description" plan-version="plan-13-v1">
  <metadata>
    <backlog-issue>PB-221</backlog-issue>
    <status>active</status> <!-- active | complete | abandoned | in-review -->
    <created>2026-04-09</created>
  </metadata>

  <tasks>
    <task id="1" risk="high" status="pending">
      <name>Task name here</name>
      <commit></commit>
      <created-from>plan-13-v1</created-from>
      <closed-at-version></closed-at-version>
      <comments>
        <comment timestamp="2026-04-09T14:00:00Z">De-risk validated: approach works.</comment>
      </comments>
      <deviations>
        <deviation type="minor" timestamp="2026-04-09T15:00:00Z">Split into two sub-tasks because...</deviation>
      </deviations>
    </task>
  </tasks>

  <project-updates>
    <update timestamp="2026-04-09T13:00:00Z">Plan ready for review.</update>
    <update timestamp="2026-04-09T16:00:00Z" blocked="true">Blocked: fundamental issue with approach X.</update>
  </project-updates>
</plan-tasks>
```

Valid task statuses: `pending`, `in-progress`, `de-risked`, `agent-coded`, `closed`, `needs-triage`, `abandoned`

## Linear operation → XML mapping

| # | Linear operation | XML equivalent |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | Create `[agent]` project | Create `plan-{N}-tasks.xml` with metadata |
| 2 | Create task issues | Add `<task>` elements |
| 3 | Update task status | Update `status` attribute on `<task>` |
| 4 | Post comment for review | Append `<comment>` to task |
| 5 | Record commit reference | Update `<commit>` element |
| 6 | Log minor deviation | Append `<deviation type="minor">` |
| 7 | Flag major deviation | Append `<update blocked="true">` at project level |
| 8 | Close task | Set status to `closed`, record closing version |
| 9 | Mark needs-triage | Set status to `needs-triage` |
| 10 | Complete/archive project | Set metadata status to `complete` |
| 11 | Record audit trail | `<created-from>` and `<closed-at-version>` per task |
| 12 | Post project update | Append `<update>` to `<project-updates>` |

## TypeScript scripts

Located at `scripts/tasks/` in the plugin repo. Each script handles one or more XML operations:

| Script | Operations covered | Example invocation |
|---|---|---|
| `init.ts` | #1, #2 | `node dist/init.js --plan 13 --tasks-from .agents/plans/plan-13.md` |
| `update-status.ts` | #3, #8, #9 | `node dist/update-status.js --plan 13 --task 2 --status agent-coded` |
| `add-comment.ts` | #4 | `node dist/add-comment.js --plan 13 --task 2 --message "De-risk validated"` |
| `set-commit.ts` | #5 | `node dist/set-commit.js --plan 13 --task 2 --commit a1b2c3d` |
| `add-deviation.ts` | #6 | `node dist/add-deviation.js --plan 13 --task 2 --type minor --message "Split task"` |
| `project-update.ts` | #7, #10, #12 | `node dist/project-update.js --plan 13 --message "Ready for review"` |
| `show.ts` | (read-only) | `node dist/show.js --plan 13` (pretty-print current state) |

All scripts read/write `.agents/plans/plan-{N}-tasks.xml` relative to the repo root.

## Plugin infrastructure

### package.json (in plugin repo root)
```json
{
  "dependencies": {
    "typescript": "^5.x",
    "fast-xml-parser": "^5.x"
  }
}
```

### tsconfig.json (in plugin repo root)
```json
{
  "compilerOptions": {
    "target": "ES2020",
    "module": "commonjs",
    "outDir": "${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA}/dist",
    "rootDir": "./scripts/tasks",
    "strict": true
  },
  "include": ["scripts/tasks/**/*.ts"]
}
```

### SessionStart hook (in hooks/hooks.json)
```json
{
  "hooks": {
    "SessionStart": [{
      "hooks": [{
        "type": "command",
        "command": "diff -q \"${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/package.json\" \"${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA}/package.json\" >/dev/null 2>&1 || (cd \"${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA}\" && cp \"${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/package.json\" . && npm install && cp \"${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/tsconfig.json\" . && npx tsc -p . && cp -r \"${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/scripts/tasks\" \"${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA}/src\") || rm -f \"${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA}/package.json\""
      }]
    }]
  }
}
```

### SKILL.md changes
- Add "Task Tracking Mode" section after Core Invariants
- Add `[Linear]`/`[Local]` markers at ~10 divergence points (enumerated in Linear operation mapping above)
- Update Core Invariant 3 for local mode: reference feature motivation in Context section
- Update Core Invariant 4: "Linear or the local tasks file tracks task state only"
- Update "What Lives Where" table with local mode row
- Update Repository Structure to show tasks.xml files

## Tasks

- Add `package.json` with `typescript` + `fast-xml-parser` dependencies
- Add `tsconfig.json`
- Create a minimal `scripts/tasks/hello.ts` test file (imports fast-xml-parser, logs success)
- Create `hooks/hooks.json` with SessionStart hook using the diff-and-install pattern

**Default Risk: High** — first hook in this plugin, untested `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA}` pattern, and the entire plan depends on this working.

- Run `node ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA}/dist/hello.js` to confirm it executes.
- If this fails, diagnose and fix before proceeding. The approach may need rethinking.

**Default Risk: High** — validation gate. If this doesn't work, everything after it is blocked.

### Task 3: Create TypeScript project infrastructure

- Shared types/interfaces for the XML schema (task statuses, risk levels, etc.)

**Default Risk: Low** — standard project setup, no unknowns.

### Task 4: Implement `init.ts` — create tasks XML from plan file

# Plan 13: Local file-based task tracking as alternative to Linear

## Context
The code-flow plugin currently depends on Linear for all task state tracking. This means users can't try the workflow without a Linear account and the Linear MCP server configured. Adding a local file-based alternative makes the plugin accessible to anyone with just Claude Code.

**Linear feature issue:** PB-221

## Design decisions

- **Format: XML** — machine-writable, extensible, not whitespace-sensitive (unlike YAML). Human readability deferred to a future viewer tool.
- **File location: `.agents/plans/plan-{N}-tasks.xml`** — sibling to the plan file. Keeps the flat directory structure, no migration needed for existing plans, compatible with existing git tag conventions.
- **Scripts: TypeScript, pre-compiled to JS** — TS source in `scripts/` in the plugin repo. SessionStart hook runs `tsc` once into `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA}/dist/`. Runtime invocation is plain `node`. TS source also copied to `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA}/src/` for browsing.
- **Dependencies: `typescript` + `fast-xml-parser`** (~26MB) — installed in `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA}` via SessionStart hook using the documented diff-and-install pattern.
- **Mode detection:** SKILL.md uses `[Linear]` / `[Local]` inline markers at divergence points. Unmarked instructions apply to both modes.

## XML schema

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<plan-tasks plan="13" description="Short description" plan-version="plan-13-v1">
  <metadata>
    <backlog-issue>PB-221</backlog-issue>
    <status>active</status> <!-- active | complete | abandoned | in-review -->
    <created>2026-04-09</created>
  </metadata>

  <tasks>
    <task id="1" risk="high" status="pending">
      <name>Task name here</name>
      <commit></commit>
      <created-from>plan-13-v1</created-from>
      <closed-at-version></closed-at-version>
      <comments>
        <comment timestamp="2026-04-09T14:00:00Z">De-risk validated: approach works.</comment>
      </comments>
      <deviations>
        <deviation type="minor" timestamp="2026-04-09T15:00:00Z">Split into two sub-tasks because...</deviation>
      </deviations>
    </task>
  </tasks>

  <project-updates>
    <update timestamp="2026-04-09T13:00:00Z">Plan ready for review.</update>
    <update timestamp="2026-04-09T16:00:00Z" blocked="true">Blocked: fundamental issue with approach X.</update>
  </project-updates>
</plan-tasks>
```

Valid task statuses: `pending`, `in-progress`, `de-risked`, `agent-coded`, `closed`, `needs-triage`, `abandoned`

## Linear operation → XML mapping

| # | Linear operation | XML equivalent |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | Create `[agent]` project | Create `plan-{N}-tasks.xml` with metadata |
| 2 | Create task issues | Add `<task>` elements |
| 3 | Update task status | Update `status` attribute on `<task>` |
| 4 | Post comment for review | Append `<comment>` to task |
| 5 | Record commit reference | Update `<commit>` element |
| 6 | Log minor deviation | Append `<deviation type="minor">` |
| 7 | Flag major deviation | Append `<update blocked="true">` at project level |
| 8 | Close task | Set status to `closed`, record closing version |
| 9 | Mark needs-triage | Set status to `needs-triage` |
| 10 | Complete/archive project | Set metadata status to `complete` |
| 11 | Record audit trail | `<created-from>` and `<closed-at-version>` per task |
| 12 | Post project update | Append `<update>` to `<project-updates>` |

## TypeScript scripts

Located at `scripts/tasks/` in the plugin repo. Each script handles one or more XML operations:

| Script | Operations covered | Example invocation |
|---|---|---|
| `init.ts` | #1, #2 | `node dist/init.js --plan 13 --tasks-from .agents/plans/plan-13.md` |
| `update-status.ts` | #3, #8, #9 | `node dist/update-status.js --plan 13 --task 2 --status agent-coded` |
| `add-comment.ts` | #4 | `node dist/add-comment.js --plan 13 --task 2 --message "De-risk validated"` |
| `set-commit.ts` | #5 | `node dist/set-commit.js --plan 13 --task 2 --commit a1b2c3d` |
| `add-deviation.ts` | #6 | `node dist/add-deviation.js --plan 13 --task 2 --type minor --message "Split task"` |
| `project-update.ts` | #7, #10, #12 | `node dist/project-update.js --plan 13 --message "Ready for review"` |
| `show.ts` | (read-only) | `node dist/show.js --plan 13` (pretty-print current state) |

All scripts read/write `.agents/plans/plan-{N}-tasks.xml` relative to the repo root.

## Plugin infrastructure

### package.json (in plugin repo root)
```json
{
  "dependencies": {
    "typescript": "^5.x",
    "fast-xml-parser": "^5.x"
  }
}
```

### tsconfig.json (in plugin repo root)
```json
{
  "compilerOptions": {
    "target": "ES2020",
    "module": "commonjs",
    "outDir": "${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA}/dist",
    "rootDir": "./scripts/tasks",
    "strict": true
  },
  "include": ["scripts/tasks/**/*.ts"]
}
```

### SessionStart hook (in hooks/hooks.json)
```json
{
  "hooks": {
    "SessionStart": [{
      "hooks": [{
        "type": "command",
        "command": "diff -q \"${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/package.json\" \"${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA}/package.json\" >/dev/null 2>&1 || (cd \"${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA}\" && cp \"${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/package.json\" . && npm install && cp \"${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/tsconfig.json\" . && npx tsc -p . && cp -r \"${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/scripts/tasks\" \"${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA}/src\") || rm -f \"${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA}/package.json\""
      }]
    }]
  }
}
```

### SKILL.md changes
- Add "Task Tracking Mode" section after Core Invariants
- Add `[Linear]`/`[Local]` markers at ~10 divergence points (enumerated in Linear operation mapping above)
- Update Core Invariant 3 for local mode: reference feature motivation in Context section
- Update Core Invariant 4: "Linear or the local tasks file tracks task state only"
- Update "What Lives Where" table with local mode row
- Update Repository Structure to show tasks.xml files

## Tasks

### Task 1: SessionStart hook — install deps and verify
- Add `package.json` with `typescript` + `fast-xml-parser` dependencies
- Create `hooks/hooks.json` with SessionStart hook using the diff-and-install pattern
- Hook installs deps into `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA}` and runs `tsc` on a minimal test file
- **Verification checkpoint:** rebuild dev plugin, start a new Claude session, confirm `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA}` contains `node_modules/`, compiled JS, and copied TS source
- This task gates everything else — no point building scripts if the hook doesn't work

**Default Risk: High** — first hook in this plugin, untested `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA}` pattern, and the entire plan depends on this working. If this fails, the approach needs rethinking.

### Task 2: Create TypeScript project infrastructure
- `tsconfig.json` targeting ES2020/commonjs
- `scripts/tasks/` directory structure
- Shared types/interfaces for the XML schema (task statuses, risk levels, etc.)

**Default Risk: Low** — standard project setup, no unknowns.

### Task 3: Implement `init.ts` — create tasks XML from plan file
- Parse plan markdown to extract task names (### Task N: pattern)
- Generate XML with proper schema, all tasks set to `pending`
- Accept risk levels as CLI args or default to unset

**Default Risk: Medium** — parsing plan markdown is the main uncertainty. Need to handle variations in plan file format.

### Task 4: Implement `update-status.ts` — change task status
- Parse XML, find task by id, update status attribute
- Validate status transitions (e.g., can't go from `closed` back to `pending`)
- Update `<closed-at-version>` when status becomes `closed`

**Default Risk: Low** — straightforward XML read/update/write.

### Task 5: Implement `add-comment.ts` and `add-deviation.ts`
- Append child elements to a task's comments/deviations sections
- Auto-generate ISO timestamps

**Default Risk: Low** — simple append operations.

### Task 6: Implement `set-commit.ts` and `project-update.ts`
- `set-commit`: update commit element on a task
- `project-update`: append to project-updates, handle `blocked` flag, update metadata status

**Default Risk: Low** — simple XML operations.

### Task 7: Implement `show.ts` — pretty-print task state
- Read XML and output a human-readable summary (task table + recent updates)
- Useful for the agent to inspect current state

**Default Risk: Low** — read-only, formatting only.

### Task 8: Update SKILL.md with `[Linear]`/`[Local]` mode markers
- Add "Task Tracking Mode" section
- Add markers at all 10+ divergence points
- Update Core Invariants 3 and 4
- Update "What Lives Where" table
- Update Repository Structure

**Default Risk: High** — largest change, highest risk of inconsistency. The SKILL.md is the contract for the entire workflow; errors here break the agent's behavior. Needs careful review.

### Task 9: Update README.md
- Note local task tracking as an alternative to Linear in Features section
- No new installation steps needed (SessionStart hook handles deps)

**Default Risk: Low** — small text change.

## Files to create/modify
- **Create:** `scripts/tasks/init.ts`, `update-status.ts`, `add-comment.ts`, `add-deviation.ts`, `set-commit.ts`, `project-update.ts`, `show.ts`, `types.ts`
- **Create:** `hooks/hooks.json`
- **Modify:** `package.json` — add dependencies
- **Create:** `tsconfig.json`
- **Modify:** `skills/code-flow/SKILL.md` — add local mode
- **Modify:** `README.md` — mention local mode

## Verification
1. Run `npm install` and `npx tsc` — confirm JS output compiles cleanly
2. Run `node dist/init.js --plan 99 --tasks-from .agents/plans/plan-12.md` — confirm valid XML generated
3. Run each script against the test XML — confirm operations produce valid XML
4. Run `mvn process-resources` — confirm SKILL.md with new sections copies to dev build
5. Enable the dev plugin, start a new session — confirm SessionStart hook installs deps and compiles TS
6. Read the modified SKILL.md end-to-end for logical consistency between Linear and Local modes

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<project xmlns="http://maven.apache.org/POM/4.0.0"
         xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"
         xsi:schemaLocation="http://maven.apache.org/POM/4.0.0 http://maven.apache.org/xsd/maven-4.0.0.xsd">
  <modelVersion>4.0.0</modelVersion>

  <groupId>com.github.pramodbiligiri</groupId>
  <artifactId>code-flow</artifactId>
  <version>0.1.0</version>
  <packaging>pom</packaging>

  <properties>
    <maven.compiler.source>21</maven.compiler.source>
    <maven.compiler.target>21</maven.compiler.target>
    <project.build.sourceEncoding>UTF-8</project.build.sourceEncoding>
  </properties>

  <profiles>
    <profile>
      <id>dev</id>
      <activation>
        <activeByDefault>true</activeByDefault>
      </activation>
      <properties>
        <plugin.name>code-flow-dev</plugin.name>
        <plugin.skillName>code-flow-dev</plugin.skillName>
        <plugin.description>Agent coding workflow (dev build)</plugin.description>
        <build.outputDir>${project.basedir}/build</build.outputDir>
      </properties>
    </profile>

    <profile>
      <id>prod</id>
      <properties>
        <plugin.name>code-flow</plugin.name>
        <plugin.skillName>code-flow</plugin.skillName>
        <plugin.description>Agent coding workflow with plan-before-implement discipline, TDD, vertical slices, Linear integration, and immutable git-based plan versioning.</plugin.description>
        <build.outputDir>${project.basedir}/build</build.outputDir>
      </properties>
    </profile>
  </profiles>

  <build>
    <plugins>
      <plugin>
        <groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId>
        <artifactId>maven-resources-plugin</artifactId>
        <version>3.3.1</version>
        <executions>
          <!-- Copy and filter SKILL.md into build/skills/{plugin.skillName}/ -->
          <execution>
            <id>copy-skill</id>
            <phase>process-resources</phase>
            <goals>
              <goal>copy-resources</goal>
            </goals>
            <configuration>
              <outputDirectory>${build.outputDir}/skills/${plugin.skillName}</outputDirectory>
              <resources>
                <resource>
                  <directory>${project.basedir}/skills/code-flow</directory>
                  <filtering>true</filtering>
                  <includes>
                    <include>SKILL.md</include>
                  </includes>
                </resource>
              </resources>
            </configuration>
          </execution>

          <!-- Copy and filter .claude-plugin/ files into build/.claude-plugin/ -->
          <execution>
            <id>copy-plugin-meta</id>
            <phase>process-resources</phase>
            <goals>
              <goal>copy-resources</goal>
            </goals>
            <configuration>
              <outputDirectory>${build.outputDir}/.claude-plugin</outputDirectory>
              <resources>
                <resource>
                  <directory>${project.basedir}/src/main/resources/claude-plugin</directory>
                  <filtering>true</filtering>
                </resource>
              </resources>
            </configuration>
          </execution>
        </executions>
      </plugin>
    </plugins>
  </build>
</project>

content.text: The `npm install` ran and `tsconfig.json` was copied, but `tsc` didn't run (or silently failed). Let me look at the hook command more carefully.

The problem: the `tsconfig.json` in `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA}` has `"rootDir": "scripts/tasks"` as a relative path — but when `tsc` runs from `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA}`, there's no `scripts/tasks/` there. The `--rootDir` override I pass on the CLI should win, but the `"include"` array in `tsconfig.json` also points to `scripts/tasks/**/*.ts` relative to the tsconfig location — so `tsc` finds no files and silently exits with no output and no error.

The fix: the tsconfig copied to `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA}` needs `include` pointing to the actual source location. Better approach — pass `--rootDir` and the source files explicitly to `tsc`, bypassing the tsconfig `include`:

The cleanest approach that actually works: symlink `node_modules` into the plugin's `scripts/tasks/` directory (or the repo root). We can do this by making the compile happen differently: instead of `rootDir` pointing to the plugin root, we symlink or copy the source into `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA}` alongside `node_modules`, then compile from there.

2. `cp -r "${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/scripts/tasks" "${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA}/src"` (copy source alongside node_modules)

This means the source is in `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA}/src`, `node_modules` in `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA}/node_modules`, and tsc finds everything by walking up from src/. This is exactly the plan we had — just the tsconfig `include` needs to point to `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA}/src`, not `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/scripts/tasks`.

      </properties>

  <build>
    <plugins>
      <plugin>

            <phase>process-resources</phase>
            <goals>
              <goal>copy-resources</goal>
            </goals>

              <outputDirectory>${build.outputDir}/hooks</outputDirectory>
              <resources>
                <resource>
                  <directory>${project.basedir}/hooks</directory>
                  <filtering>false</filtering>
                </resource>
              </resources>
            </configuration>
          </execution>

          <!-- Copy scripts/tasks/ into build/scripts/tasks/ -->
          <execution>
            <id>copy-scripts</id>
            <phase>process-resources</phase>
            <goals>
              <goal>copy-resources</goal>
            </goals>
            <configuration>
              <outputDirectory>${build.outputDir}/scripts/tasks</outputDirectory>
              <resources>
                <resource>
                  <directory>${project.basedir}/scripts/tasks</directory>
                  <filtering>false</filtering>
                </resource>
              </resources>
            </configuration>
          </execution>

          <!-- Copy package.json and tsconfig.json into build/ -->
          <execution>
            <id>copy-node-config</id>
            <phase>process-resources</phase>
            <goals>
              <goal>copy-resources</goal>
            </goals>
            <configuration>
              <outputDirectory>${build.outputDir}</outputDirectory>
              <resources>

                  <directory>${project.basedir}</directory>
                  <filtering>false</filtering>
                  <includes>
                    <include>package.json</include>
                    <include>tsconfig.json</include>
                  </includes>
                </resource>
              </resources>
            </configuration>
          </execution>
        </executions>
      </plugin>
    </plugins>
  </build>
</project>

content.text:
The verbose output says `copy-node-config` copies "1 resource from  to build" — the directory is blank, meaning it's copying from the project base dir. Maven's `copy-resources` with `<directory>${project.basedir}</directory>` and `<includes><include>package.json</include></includes>` should only copy `package.json`, but it seems to also be picking up `tsconfig.json`. 

This is a Maven quirk — when the `<directory>` is the project root, it may be including more than just the `<includes>` specify. The simplest fix is to move `package.json` to a dedicated subdirectory (e.g. `src/main/node/`) so Maven copies cleanly from there.

Actually, `tsconfig.json` in the build isn't harmful — the hook doesn't use it. But it's noise. Let me not get distracted by this and focus on what matters: the hook works correctly. The `tsconfig.json` in `build/` is harmless. 

Let me now commit the fixed hook and rebuild:

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Plan 13: Local file-based task tracking as alternative to Linear

## Context
The code-flow plugin currently depends on Linear for all task state tracking. This means users can't try the workflow without a Linear account and the Linear MCP server configured. Adding a local file-based alternative makes the plugin accessible to anyone with just Claude Code.

**Linear feature issue:** PB-221

## Design decisions

- **Format: XML** — machine-writable, extensible, not whitespace-sensitive (unlike YAML). Human readability deferred to a future viewer tool.
- **File location: `.agents/plans/plan-{N}-tasks.xml`** — sibling to the plan file. Keeps the flat directory structure, no migration needed for existing plans, compatible with existing git tag conventions.
- **Scripts: TypeScript, pre-compiled to JS** — TS source in `scripts/tasks/` in the plugin repo. SessionStart hook runs `tsc` once into `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA}/dist/`. Runtime invocation is plain `node`. TS source also copied to `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA}/src/` for browsing.
- **Dependencies: `typescript` + `fast-xml-parser`** (~26MB) — installed in `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA}` via SessionStart hook using the documented diff-and-install pattern from the [plugins reference](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/plugins-reference#persistent-data-directory).
- **Mode detection:** SKILL.md uses `[Linear]` / `[Local]` inline markers at divergence points. Unmarked instructions apply to both modes.

## XML schema

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<plan-tasks plan="13" description="Short description" plan-version="plan-13-v1">
  <metadata>
    <backlog-issue>PB-221</backlog-issue>
    <status>active</status> <!-- active | complete | abandoned | in-review -->
    <created>2026-04-09</created>
  </metadata>

  <tasks>
    <task id="1" risk="high" status="pending">
      <name>Task name here</name>
      <commit></commit>
      <created-from>plan-13-v1</created-from>
      <closed-at-version></closed-at-version>
      <comments>
        <comment timestamp="2026-04-09T14:00:00Z">De-risk validated: approach works.</comment>
      </comments>
      <deviations>
        <deviation type="minor" timestamp="2026-04-09T15:00:00Z">Split into two sub-tasks because...</deviation>
      </deviations>
    </task>
  </tasks>

  <project-updates>
    <update timestamp="2026-04-09T13:00:00Z">Plan ready for review.</update>
    <update timestamp="2026-04-09T16:00:00Z" blocked="true">Blocked: fundamental issue with approach X.</update>
  </project-updates>
</plan-tasks>
```

Valid task statuses: `pending`, `in-progress`, `de-risked`, `agent-coded`, `closed`, `needs-triage`, `abandoned`

## Linear operation → XML mapping

| # | Linear operation | XML equivalent |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | Create `[agent]` project | Create `plan-{N}-tasks.xml` with metadata |
| 2 | Create task issues | Add `<task>` elements |
| 3 | Update task status | Update `status` attribute on `<task>` |
| 4 | Post comment for review | Append `<comment>` to task |
| 5 | Record commit reference | Update `<commit>` element |
| 6 | Log minor deviation | Append `<deviation type="minor">` |
| 7 | Flag major deviation | Append `<update blocked="true">` at project level |
| 8 | Close task | Set status to `closed`, record closing version |
| 9 | Mark needs-triage | Set status to `needs-triage` |
| 10 | Complete/archive project | Set metadata status to `complete` |
| 11 | Record audit trail | `<created-from>` and `<closed-at-version>` per task |
| 12 | Post project update | Append `<update>` to `<project-updates>` |

## TypeScript scripts

Located at `scripts/tasks/` in the plugin repo. Each script handles one or more XML operations:

| Script | Operations covered | Example invocation |
|---|---|---|
| `init.ts` | #1, #2 | `node dist/init.js --plan 13 --tasks-from .agents/plans/plan-13.md` |
| `update-status.ts` | #3, #8, #9 | `node dist/update-status.js --plan 13 --task 2 --status agent-coded` |
| `add-comment.ts` | #4 | `node dist/add-comment.js --plan 13 --task 2 --message "De-risk validated"` |
| `set-commit.ts` | #5 | `node dist/set-commit.js --plan 13 --task 2 --commit a1b2c3d` |
| `add-deviation.ts` | #6 | `node dist/add-deviation.js --plan 13 --task 2 --type minor --message "Split task"` |
| `project-update.ts` | #7, #10, #12 | `node dist/project-update.js --plan 13 --message "Ready for review"` |
| `show.ts` | (read-only) | `node dist/show.js --plan 13` (pretty-print current state) |

All scripts read/write `.agents/plans/plan-{N}-tasks.xml` relative to the repo root.

## Plugin infrastructure

### package.json (in plugin repo root)
```json
{
  "dependencies": {
    "typescript": "^5.x",
    "fast-xml-parser": "^5.x"
  }
}
```

### tsconfig.json (in plugin repo root)
```json
{
  "compilerOptions": {
    "target": "ES2020",
    "module": "commonjs",
    "outDir": "${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA}/dist",
    "rootDir": "./scripts/tasks",
    "strict": true
  },
  "include": ["scripts/tasks/**/*.ts"]
}
```

### SessionStart hook (in hooks/hooks.json)
```json
{
  "hooks": {
    "SessionStart": [{
      "hooks": [{
        "type": "command",
        "command": "diff -q \"${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/package.json\" \"${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA}/package.json\" >/dev/null 2>&1 || (cd \"${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA}\" && cp \"${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/package.json\" . && npm install && cp \"${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/tsconfig.json\" . && npx tsc -p . && cp -r \"${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/scripts/tasks\" \"${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA}/src\") || rm -f \"${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA}/package.json\""
      }]
    }]
  }
}
```

### SKILL.md changes
- Add "Task Tracking Mode" section after Core Invariants
- Add `[Linear]`/`[Local]` markers at ~10 divergence points (enumerated in Linear operation mapping above)
- Update Core Invariant 3 for local mode: reference feature motivation in Context section
- Update Core Invariant 4: "Linear or the local tasks file tracks task state only"
- Update "What Lives Where" table with local mode row
- Update Repository Structure to show tasks.xml files

## Tasks (risk-sorted: High → Medium → Low)

### Task 1: SessionStart hook — install deps and compile [High]
- Add `package.json` with `typescript` + `fast-xml-parser` dependencies
- Add `tsconfig.json`
- Create a minimal `scripts/tasks/hello.ts` test file (imports fast-xml-parser, logs success)
- Create `hooks/hooks.json` with SessionStart hook using the diff-and-install pattern
- Rebuild dev plugin (`mvn process-resources`)

### Task 2: Manually verify deps installation [High]
- **Human task.** Start a new Claude session with the dev plugin enabled.
- Confirm `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA}` contains: `node_modules/`, compiled `dist/hello.js`, and `src/` with TS source copy.
- Run `node ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA}/dist/hello.js` to confirm it executes.
- If this fails, diagnose and fix before proceeding. The approach may need rethinking.
- **Nothing proceeds until this passes.**

### Task 3: Update SKILL.md with `[Linear]`/`[Local]` mode markers [High]
- Add "Task Tracking Mode" section
- Add markers at all 10+ divergence points
- Update Core Invariants 3 and 4
- Update "What Lives Where" table
- Update Repository Structure

### Task 4: Implement `init.ts` — create tasks XML from plan file [Medium]
- Parse plan markdown to extract task names (`### Task N:` pattern)
- Generate XML with proper schema, all tasks set to `pending`
- Accept risk levels as CLI args or default to unset

### Task 5: Create TypeScript project infrastructure [Low]
- `scripts/tasks/` directory structure
- Shared types/interfaces for the XML schema (task statuses, risk levels, etc.)

### Task 6: Implement `update-status.ts` — change task status [Low]
- Parse XML, find task by id, update status attribute
- Validate status transitions
- Update `<closed-at-version>` when status becomes `closed`

### Task 7: Implement `add-comment.ts` and `add-deviation.ts` [Low]

### Task 8: Implement `set-commit.ts` and `project-update.ts` [Low]

### Task 9: Implement `show.ts` — pretty-print task state [Low]

### Task 10: Update README.md [Low]
- Note local task tracking as an alternative to Linear in Features section

## Files to create/modify
- **Create:** `scripts/tasks/hello.ts` (test file, later replaced by real scripts)
- **Create:** `scripts/tasks/types.ts`, `init.ts`, `update-status.ts`, `add-comment.ts`, `add-deviation.ts`, `set-commit.ts`, `project-update.ts`, `show.ts`

## Verification
1. Rebuild dev plugin (`mvn process-resources`) after Task 1
2. Start new Claude session — confirm SessionStart hook installs deps and compiles TS (Task 2)
3. Run `node dist/init.js --plan 99 --tasks-from .agents/plans/plan-12.md` — confirm valid XML generated
4. Run each script against test XML — confirm operations produce valid XML
5. Run `mvn process-resources` — confirm SKILL.md with new sections copies to dev build

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Plan 13: Local file-based task tracking as alternative to Linear

## Context
The code-flow plugin currently depends on Linear for all task state tracking. This means users can't try the workflow without a Linear account and the Linear MCP server configured. Adding a local file-based alternative makes the plugin accessible to anyone with just Claude Code.

**Linear feature issue:** PB-221

## Design decisions

- **Format: XML** — machine-writable, extensible, not whitespace-sensitive (unlike YAML). Human readability deferred to a future viewer tool.
- **File location: `.agents/plans/plan-{N}-tasks.xml`** — sibling to the plan file. Keeps the flat directory structure, no migration needed for existing plans, compatible with existing git tag conventions.
- **Scripts: TypeScript, pre-compiled to JS** — TS source in `scripts/tasks/` in the plugin repo. SessionStart hook runs `tsc` once into `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA}/dist/`. Runtime invocation is plain `node`. TS source also copied to `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA}/src/` for browsing.
- **Dependencies: `typescript` + `fast-xml-parser`** (~26MB) — installed in `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA}` via SessionStart hook using the documented diff-and-install pattern from the [plugins reference](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/plugins-reference#persistent-data-directory).
- **Mode detection:** SKILL.md uses `[Linear]` / `[Local]` inline markers at divergence points. Unmarked instructions apply to both modes.

## XML schema

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<plan-tasks plan="13" description="Short description" plan-version="plan-13-v1">
  <metadata>
    <backlog-issue>PB-221</backlog-issue>
    <status>active</status> <!-- active | complete | abandoned | in-review -->
    <created>2026-04-09</created>
  </metadata>

  <tasks>
    <task id="1" risk="high" status="pending">
      <name>Task name here</name>
      <commit></commit>
      <created-from>plan-13-v1</created-from>
      <closed-at-version></closed-at-version>
      <comments>
        <comment timestamp="2026-04-09T14:00:00Z">De-risk validated: approach works.</comment>
      </comments>
      <deviations>
        <deviation type="minor" timestamp="2026-04-09T15:00:00Z">Split into two sub-tasks because...</deviation>
      </deviations>
    </task>
  </tasks>

  <project-updates>
    <update timestamp="2026-04-09T13:00:00Z">Plan ready for review.</update>
    <update timestamp="2026-04-09T16:00:00Z" blocked="true">Blocked: fundamental issue with approach X.</update>
  </project-updates>
</plan-tasks>
```

Valid task statuses: `pending`, `in-progress`, `de-risked`, `agent-coded`, `closed`, `needs-triage`, `abandoned`

## Linear operation → XML mapping

| # | Linear operation | XML equivalent |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | Create `[agent]` project | Create `plan-{N}-tasks.xml` with metadata |
| 2 | Create task issues | Add `<task>` elements |
| 3 | Update task status | Update `status` attribute on `<task>` |
| 4 | Post comment for review | Append `<comment>` to task |
| 5 | Record commit reference | Update `<commit>` element |
| 6 | Log minor deviation | Append `<deviation type="minor">` |
| 7 | Flag major deviation | Append `<update blocked="true">` at project level |
| 8 | Close task | Set status to `closed`, record closing version |
| 9 | Mark needs-triage | Set status to `needs-triage` |
| 10 | Complete/archive project | Set metadata status to `complete` |
| 11 | Record audit trail | `<created-from>` and `<closed-at-version>` per task |
| 12 | Post project update | Append `<update>` to `<project-updates>` |

## TypeScript scripts

Located at `scripts/tasks/` in the plugin repo. Each script handles one or more XML operations:

| Script | Operations covered | Example invocation |
|---|---|---|
| `init.ts` | #1, #2 | `node dist/init.js --plan 13 --tasks-from .agents/plans/plan-13.md` |
| `update-status.ts` | #3, #8, #9 | `node dist/update-status.js --plan 13 --task 2 --status agent-coded` |
| `add-comment.ts` | #4 | `node dist/add-comment.js --plan 13 --task 2 --message "De-risk validated"` |
| `set-commit.ts` | #5 | `node dist/set-commit.js --plan 13 --task 2 --commit a1b2c3d` |
| `add-deviation.ts` | #6 | `node dist/add-deviation.js --plan 13 --task 2 --type minor --message "Split task"` |
| `project-update.ts` | #7, #10, #12 | `node dist/project-update.js --plan 13 --message "Ready for review"` |
| `show.ts` | (read-only) | `node dist/show.js --plan 13` (pretty-print current state) |

All scripts read/write `.agents/plans/plan-{N}-tasks.xml` relative to the repo root.

## Plugin infrastructure

### package.json (in plugin repo root)
```json
{
  "dependencies": {
    "typescript": "^5.x",
    "fast-xml-parser": "^5.x"
  }
}
```

### tsconfig.json (in plugin repo root)
```json
{
  "compilerOptions": {
    "target": "ES2020",
    "module": "commonjs",
    "outDir": "${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA}/dist",
    "rootDir": "./scripts/tasks",
    "strict": true
  },
  "include": ["scripts/tasks/**/*.ts"]
}
```

### SessionStart hook (in hooks/hooks.json)
```json
{
  "hooks": {
    "SessionStart": [{
      "hooks": [{
        "type": "command",
        "command": "diff -q \"${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/package.json\" \"${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA}/package.json\" >/dev/null 2>&1 || (cd \"${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA}\" && cp \"${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/package.json\" . && npm install && cp \"${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/tsconfig.json\" . && npx tsc -p . && cp -r \"${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/scripts/tasks\" \"${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA}/src\") || rm -f \"${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA}/package.json\""
      }]
    }]
  }
}
```

### SKILL.md changes
- Add "Task Tracking Mode" section after Core Invariants
- Add `[Linear]`/`[Local]` markers at ~10 divergence points (enumerated in Linear operation mapping above)
- Update Core Invariant 3 for local mode: reference feature motivation in Context section
- Update Core Invariant 4: "Linear or the local tasks file tracks task state only"
- Update "What Lives Where" table with local mode row
- Update Repository Structure to show tasks.xml files

## Tasks (risk-sorted: High → Medium → Low)

### Task 1: SessionStart hook — install deps and compile [High]
- Add `package.json` with `typescript` + `fast-xml-parser` dependencies
- Add `tsconfig.json`
- Create a minimal `scripts/tasks/hello.ts` test file (imports fast-xml-parser, logs success)
- Create `hooks/hooks.json` with SessionStart hook using the diff-and-install pattern
- Rebuild dev plugin (`mvn process-resources`)

### Task 2: Manually verify deps installation [High]
- **Human task.** Start a new Claude session with the dev plugin enabled.
- Confirm `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA}` contains: `node_modules/`, compiled `dist/hello.js`, and `src/` with TS source copy.
- Run `node ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA}/dist/hello.js` to confirm it executes.
- If this fails, diagnose and fix before proceeding. The approach may need rethinking.
- **Nothing proceeds until this passes.**

### Task 3: Update SKILL.md with `[Linear]`/`[Local]` mode markers [High]
- Add "Task Tracking Mode" section
- Add markers at all 10+ divergence points
- Update Core Invariants 3 and 4
- Update "What Lives Where" table
- Update Repository Structure

### Task 4: Implement `init.ts` — create tasks XML from plan file [Medium]
- Parse plan markdown to extract task names (`### Task N:` pattern)
- Generate XML with proper schema, all tasks set to `pending`
- Accept risk levels as CLI args or default to unset

### Task 5: Create TypeScript project infrastructure [Low]
- `scripts/tasks/` directory structure
- Shared types/interfaces for the XML schema (task statuses, risk levels, etc.)

### Task 6: Implement `update-status.ts` — change task status [Low]
- Parse XML, find task by id, update status attribute
- Validate status transitions
- Update `<closed-at-version>` when status becomes `closed`

### Task 7: Implement `add-comment.ts` and `add-deviation.ts` [Low]
- Append child elements to a task's comments/deviations sections
- Auto-generate ISO timestamps

### Task 8: Implement `set-commit.ts` and `project-update.ts` [Low]
- `set-commit`: update commit element on a task
- `project-update`: append to project-updates, handle `blocked` flag, update metadata status

### Task 9: Implement `show.ts` — pretty-print task state [Low]
- Read XML and output a human-readable summary (task table + recent updates)

### Task 10: Fix tsconfig.json leaking into build output [Low]
- Maven's `copy-node-config` execution copies `tsconfig.json` into `build/` unintentionally
  (Maven scans the project root directory even with explicit `<includes>`)
- Fix: move `package.json` to `src/main/node/` so Maven copies cleanly from a dedicated dir
- Verify `tsconfig.json` no longer appears in `build/` after `mvn clean process-resources`

### Task 11: Update README.md [Low]
- Note local task tracking as an alternative to Linear in Features section

## Files to create/modify
- **Create:** `scripts/tasks/hello.ts` (test file, later replaced by real scripts)
- **Create:** `scripts/tasks/types.ts`, `init.ts`, `update-status.ts`, `add-comment.ts`, `add-deviation.ts`, `set-commit.ts`, `project-update.ts`, `show.ts`
- **Create:** `hooks/hooks.json`
- **Modify:** `package.json` — add dependencies
- **Create:** `tsconfig.json`
- **Modify:** `skills/code-flow/SKILL.md` — add local mode
- **Modify:** `README.md` — mention local mode

## Verification
1. Rebuild dev plugin (`mvn process-resources`) after Task 1
2. Start new Claude session — confirm SessionStart hook installs deps and compiles TS (Task 2)
3. Run `node dist/init.js --plan 99 --tasks-from .agents/plans/plan-12.md` — confirm valid XML generated
4. Run each script against test XML — confirm operations produce valid XML
5. Run `mvn process-resources` — confirm SKILL.md with new sections copies to dev build
6. Read modified SKILL.md end-to-end for logical consistency between Linear and Local modes

input.title: Fix tsconfig.json leaking into build output
input.description:
**Risk:** Low
**Plan:** [plan-13-v2](https://github.com/pramodbiligiri/code-flow/blob/8836bc853e1d726262b00873715dd1a231304472/.agents/plans/plan-13.md)

Maven's `copy-node-config` execution copies `tsconfig.json` into `build/` unintentionally — Maven scans the project root even with explicit `<includes>`. Risk: `tsconfig.json` could be inadvertently packaged into the distributed plugin.

Fix: move `package.json` to `src/main/node/` so Maven copies cleanly from a dedicated directory.

Verify `tsconfig.json` no longer appears in `build/` after `mvn clean process-resources`.

message.content: Show me instructions again

content.text:
Here are the verification steps for Task 2 (PB-223):

1. **Rebuild the dev plugin** (already done, but run again to be sure):
   ```bash
   cd /opt/workspace/code-flow && mvn clean process-resources -q
   ```

2. **Start a new Claude Code session** with the dev plugin enabled. The SessionStart hook fires automatically on session start.

3. **Check the data directory**:
   ```bash
   ls ~/.claude/plugins/data/code-flow-dev-code-flow-dev/
   ```
   Expected: `node_modules/  package.json  package-lock.json  src/  dist/`

4. **Verify compiled output exists**:
   ```bash
   ls ~/.claude/plugins/data/code-flow-dev-code-flow-dev/dist/
   ```
   Expected: `hello.js`

5. **Run it**:
   ```bash
   node ~/.claude/plugins/data/code-flow-dev-code-flow-dev/dist/hello.js
   ```
   Expected output:
   ```
   code-flow task scripts: deps OK, fast-xml-parser loaded successfully
   Parsed XML: {"hello":"world"}
   ```

If any step fails, run the hook command manually to see the error:
```bash
CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT=/path/to/build CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA=~/.claude/plugins/data/code-flow-dev-code-flow-dev bash -c '<paste hook command here>'
```
